goal: Migrate OpenNori toward the TypeScript agent state CLI architecture while preserving its acceptance-driven product boundary
summary: Use strict TypeScript, a citty command layer, public JSON Schema with Ajv validation, tsc builds without bundling, Vitest and Biome quality gates, manifest-managed lifecycle actions, directory-based Skills, and build-vs-buy checks before custom infrastructure.
applies_to: Codex Plugin-packaged Skills and manifest managed state

sources.label: OpenAI Codex Agent Skills
sources.lesson: Skills are directory-based capability units with SKILL.md plus optional references, scripts, assets, and UI metadata; OpenNori ships Skills as package assets instead of JS string prompts.

sources.label: CodeGraph / GitNexus
sources.lesson: Expose deterministic agent-readable context, status, doctor, and staleness signals; use strict TypeScript and tested CLI boundaries without becoming a code graph platform.

sources.label: Compound Engineering Plugin
sources.lesson: Use lightweight TypeScript CLI architecture and citty-style command modules, while avoiding process-centered multi-agent review.

sources.label: ECC
sources.lesson: Use manifest/profile/component/install-state style productization without becoming a workflow OS.

sources.label: vibecode-pro-max-kit
sources.lesson: Borrow Skill directory organization and managed-file install boundaries, but reject RIPER phase folders and process/ as the mainline.

checks.statement: CLI command definitions are thin citty command modules that delegate product decisions to domain modules.
checks.review: Inspect src/cli/commands, command handlers, and domain modules before claiming architecture complete.

checks.statement: Persisted OpenNori state has public JSON Schema and Ajv runtime validation; OpenNori semantic rules stay separate from structural schema validation.
checks.review: Inspect schemas/ and src/validation before claiming protocol validation complete.

checks.statement: OpenNori Skill content lives in plugin-packaged plugins/opennori/skills/*/SKILL.md with optional references/scripts/assets/openai metadata, not as hard-coded JS strings or project-local copies.
checks.review: Inspect .agents/plugins/marketplace.json, plugins/opennori/.codex-plugin/plugin.json, plugins/opennori/skills/ assets, package files, and doctor plugin output.

checks.statement: Install, upgrade, uninstall, managed files, and doctor recovery are lifecycle concerns with deterministic plans, dry-run preview, and explicit confirmation boundaries.
checks.review: Inspect lifecycle plan/apply modules, manifest state, and contract tests.

checks.statement: Before custom infrastructure work, check current project dependencies, standard libraries, official SDKs, mature open-source libraries, and documented reference projects.
checks.review: Record a build-vs-buy decision or challenge before self-building infrastructure.

checks.statement: Do not replace this baseline silently. Raise an Architecture Challenge when project evidence conflicts with it.

runtime_topology.name: agent-skill-layer
runtime_topology.decision: Codex loads package-provided OpenNori Skills from the Codex Plugin and uses them to translate natural language into deterministic CLI state operations.
runtime_topology.reason: Skills hold subjective product and architecture judgment; the CLI must not become a hard-coded product-quality oracle.

runtime_topology.name: cli-state-layer
runtime_topology.decision: The opennori CLI is the deterministic state boundary for contracts, evidence, profile, architecture, lifecycle, report, and dashboard snapshot operations.
runtime_topology.reason: The CLI gives agents stable JSON and gives humans short TTY summaries without requiring users to remember internal flags.

runtime_topology.name: project-state-layer
runtime_topology.decision: .opennori is the project-local source for Nori Contracts, evidence ledgers, profiles, architecture baselines, reports, events, activity, and snapshots.
runtime_topology.reason: Project state must travel with the project and remain inspectable outside the chat session.

runtime_topology.name: dashboard-observation-layer
runtime_topology.decision: The dashboard kernel only reads state, events, activity, and snapshots and streams observation data to the local dashboard.
runtime_topology.reason: The dashboard must not become an agent runtime, confirmation surface, or Product AC evidence source.

source_of_truth.name: json-contract-state
source_of_truth.decision: Structured JSON files under .opennori are authoritative for protocol state; Markdown is generated review surface or human-readable supplement.
source_of_truth.reason: Agents and tools need deterministic state while users need readable reports.

source_of_truth.name: schema-validation-boundary
source_of_truth.decision: Public JSON Schema plus Ajv validate objective protocol shape; subjective quality remains a Skill and user-review responsibility.
source_of_truth.reason: Architecture and AC quality are contextual judgments and must not become brittle word-list validators.

source_of_truth.name: generated-projections
source_of_truth.decision: .opennori/events, activity, snapshots, reports, and baseline markdown are projections or review surfaces unless explicitly documented as authoritative state.
source_of_truth.reason: Projection files must not replace contract, evidence, profile, or architecture JSON.

module_boundaries.name: src/cli
module_boundaries.decision: Owns citty command definitions, argument normalization, human-vs-json output routing, and command composition only.
module_boundaries.reason: CLI modules should stay thin and delegate OpenNori semantics to domain modules.

module_boundaries.name: src/core
module_boundaries.decision: Owns contracts, evidence ledgers, profiles, reports, recommendation state, and shared project-state helpers.
module_boundaries.reason: Acceptance-loop semantics must remain reusable across CLI, reports, and dashboard projections.

module_boundaries.name: src/architecture
module_boundaries.decision: Owns architecture profiles, baselines, build-vs-buy decisions, challenges, apply records, agent surfaces, and architecture report sections.
module_boundaries.reason: Architecture guidance is separate from Product AC but participates in completion confidence.

module_boundaries.name: src/lifecycle
module_boundaries.decision: Owns setup, init, install, upgrade, uninstall, manifest, doctor, managed-file plans, profile checks, context export, and plugin sync.
module_boundaries.reason: Installation and recovery safety need dry-run, confirm, ownership, and recovery boundaries.

module_boundaries.name: src/kernel-and-dashboard
module_boundaries.decision: Kernel modules own event/activity/snapshot projections and dashboard transport; dashboard frontend owns visual observation only.
module_boundaries.reason: Live observation must not be confused with workflow authority or evidence.

module_boundaries.name: plugins/opennori/skills
module_boundaries.decision: Packaged Skills own agent behavior protocols, natural-language routing, subjective AC/evidence/architecture judgment, and misuse guards.
module_boundaries.reason: OpenNori is Skill-driven; users should not learn CLI internals.

contract_surfaces.name: cli-json
contract_surfaces.decision: Every agent-facing command keeps stable JSON with ok/data/artifacts/warnings/next_actions and agent_next where routing matters.
contract_surfaces.reason: Skills need deterministic state and next-step routing.

contract_surfaces.name: human-tty
contract_surfaces.decision: TTY commands without --json use concise human summaries for lifecycle, status, report, dashboard, and plugin sync flows.
contract_surfaces.reason: Human users should not receive large JSON blobs for normal operations.

contract_surfaces.name: schemas
contract_surfaces.decision: Protocol objects that are persisted or exchanged across commands have JSON Schema coverage.
contract_surfaces.reason: Schema coverage protects objective compatibility without judging product quality.

contract_surfaces.name: plugin-skill-assets
contract_surfaces.decision: Codex Plugin metadata points to directory-based Skills; lifecycle commands do not copy OpenNori Skills into user projects.
contract_surfaces.reason: Plugin-first distribution keeps OpenNori as one capability bundle.

data_flows.name: natural-language-to-contract
steps: User states a goal in natural language.
Codex loads OpenNori Skills through Plugin discovery.
Skill asks only completion-changing questions and calls draft/approve commands.
CLI writes a standard draft or current Nori Contract under .opennori.
User approves or revises before implementation.

data_flows.name: architecture-before-implementation
steps: Agent reads current goal, Product AC, Nori Profile, project evidence, and references.
Skill selects or creates an Architecture Profile with product charter and concrete technical baseline.
CLI previews a baseline without side effects.
User confirms or revises the baseline.
CLI writes baseline JSON/Markdown and agent-readable route surfaces.

data_flows.name: gap-to-evidence
steps: Agent reads current gap and confirmed architecture baseline.
Agent records architecture apply context when architecture matters.
Agent implements only the current Product AC gap.
Agent records reviewable Product AC evidence with sources, basis, confidence, and limitations.
Status/report compute completion and review risks.

data_flows.name: dashboard-observation
steps: CLI and Skills publish activity/events/snapshots as observation signals.
Kernel reads .opennori state and streams snapshots/events.
Dashboard renders current goal, gap, agent activity, architecture decision, and completion decision.
Any user decision is routed back to the agent conversation and recorded through CLI state commands.

dependency_decisions.name: TypeScript
dependency_decisions.decision: Use strict TypeScript source and tsc-emitted JavaScript for the published npm runtime.
dependency_decisions.reason: Types keep protocol and CLI boundaries coherent while npm users run plain JavaScript output.

dependency_decisions.name: citty
dependency_decisions.decision: Use citty for the command tree and command modules.
dependency_decisions.reason: Nested CLI growth should not be handled by handwritten argument parsing.

dependency_decisions.name: Ajv-and-JSON-Schema
dependency_decisions.decision: Use JSON Schema as public protocol documentation and Ajv for runtime validation.
dependency_decisions.reason: The protocol is inspectable by users and agents, and objective validation stays separate from subjective review.

dependency_decisions.name: Vitest
dependency_decisions.decision: Use Vitest for CLI, domain, lifecycle, and dashboard-kernel behavior tests.
dependency_decisions.reason: Tests should cover objective state behavior without encoding subjective AC quality.

dependency_decisions.name: Hono-dashboard-kernel
dependency_decisions.decision: Use Hono and @hono/node-server for local dashboard routing and SSE transport.
dependency_decisions.reason: Routing and streaming are solved infrastructure; OpenNori should own domain projections, not HTTP glue.

dependency_decisions.name: React-dashboard
dependency_decisions.decision: Use React, Vite, Tailwind, Radix primitives, Motion, and lucide-react for the dashboard UI.
dependency_decisions.reason: The dashboard needs responsive live visual state, accessible primitives, animation, and icons without becoming a control plane.

reference_mappings.name: OpenAI Codex Skills
reference_mappings.decision: Map directory-based Skills and Plugin discovery into plugins/opennori/skills and .codex-plugin metadata.
reference_mappings.reason: OpenNori must be agent-discoverable without project-local Skill copies.

reference_mappings.name: vibecode-pro-max-kit
reference_mappings.decision: Reuse the Skill directory and managed install insight, reject process/phase workflow as OpenNori mainline.

reference_mappings.name: ECC
reference_mappings.decision: Map profiles/components/manifest/doctor thinking into Nori Profile, lifecycle manifest, doctor, and Architecture Profile review.
reference_mappings.reason: Productized recovery and capability checks matter, but they must serve the acceptance loop.

reference_mappings.name: CLI-Anything
reference_mappings.decision: Map agent-native tool wrapping into stable CLI JSON, Skill guidance, and context export.
reference_mappings.reason: Agents need deterministic command contracts rather than prose-only instructions.

reference_mappings.name: CodeGraph/GitNexus
reference_mappings.decision: Borrow context/status/search/doctor discipline, not a full symbol graph platform.
reference_mappings.reason: OpenNori needs completion context and evidence health without becoming code intelligence infrastructure.

preferred_libraries.policy: Use TypeScript strict mode for source modules and keep generated JavaScript as npm runtime output.
Use citty for command definitions; keep commander as a fallback only if packaging, nested command, or compatibility evidence blocks citty.
Use @clack/prompts for minimal project-aware quickstart prompts; keep all state commands JSON-capable.
Use tsc emit without bundling by default; add tsdown or tsup only when a real packaging need appears.
Use public JSON Schema files as protocol truth and Ajv for runtime validation.
Use yaml for frontmatter when needed; keep JSON authoritative and avoid micromark/unified unless Markdown import requires it.
Use Vitest plus tsc --noEmit; CLI integration tests should spawn the real bin against temporary projects.
Use Biome for lint and format; agents must not weaken quality config to pass checks.
Ship directory-based Skills as Codex Plugin package assets; project lifecycle commands manage .opennori state, not project-local Skill copies.

avoid: phase/task-list state as product completion state
agent self-summary as the only high-risk completion evidence
Markdown as authoritative protocol state when JSON state exists
hard-coded Skill bodies in JS strings
bundler-first builds before tsc emit proves insufficient
external review tools as OpenNori's control plane
